Three farmers sell chickens at a market. One has 10 chickens, another has 16,
and the third has 26. Each farmer sells at least one, but not all, of his
chickens before noon, all farmers selling at the same price per chicken. Later
in the day each sells his remaining chickens, all again selling at the same
reduced price. If each farmer received a total of \$35 from the sale of his
chickens, what was the selling price before noon and the selling price after
noon? (From ``Math Can Be Fun'' by Ya Perelman.)
